items.title: Drake Maye on Mike Vrabel: Just thankful he's our head coach
items.body_text: The attention paid to the fallout from the publication of photos showing Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and NFL reporter Dianna Russini together makes it inevitable that Patriots players are going to be asked questions about the impact on their preparations for the 2026 season.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Chris Ballard on drafting Seth McGowan despite legal history: "I'm a second chance guy"
items.body_text: When Seth McGowan was a freshman running back at Oklahoma in 2020, he was arrested for stealing marijuana, jewelry and cash, pleaded guilty, served three months in jail, and was kicked off the team.
items.author: Michael David Smith

items.title: Fernando Mendoza on his leadership: I'm not always a nice guy, I'm an a-hole sometimes
items.body_text: The Raiders' new franchise quarterback says he'll demand the best of his teammates.
items.author: Michael David Smith

items.title: Giants agree to terms with DT Leki Fotu
items.body_text: The Giants are signing defensive tackle Leki Fotu to a one-year contract, according to Jordan Schultz of The Schultz Report.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Dolphins part ways with executive Champ Kelly, hire Josh Scobey
items.body_text: The Dolphins have parted ways with senior personnel executive Champ Kelly, Jonathan Jones of CBS reports.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Vikings sign DL Eric Johnson
items.body_text: The Vikings signed defensive lineman Eric Johnson on Wednesday, the team announced.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Art Rooney on Aaron Rodgers: I thought we'd know by now, think we'll know in a few weeks
items.body_text: Steelers owner Art Rooney II said a month ago that he expected to know whether Aaron Rodgers would play in Pittsburgh this season before the draft.
items.author: Michael David Smith

items.title: Steelers shift mandatory minicamp, possibly altering the Aaron Rodgers timeline
items.body_text: Last year, the Steelers' offseason program meshed perfectly with the priorities of quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Seahawks sign TE Harrison Bryant
items.body_text: The Seahawks signed tight end Harrison Bryant on Wednesday, the team announced.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: George Pickens signs franchise tag
items.body_text: The Cowboys placed the tag on the Pro Bowl wide receiver, guaranteeing him $27.3 million in 2026.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Bills sign FB Ben VanSumeren to a one-year deal
items.body_text: The Bills signed fullback Ben VanSumeren to a one-year deal on Wednesday, the team announced.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Report: Azeez Al-Shaair's three-year extension is worth $54 million
items.body_text: Texans linebacker Azeez Al-Shaair was headed into a contract season.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Chiefs waive RB ShunDerrick Powell
items.body_text: The Chiefs opened up a spot on their 90-man roster on Wednesday.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: NFL releases offseason program workout dates
items.body_text: The NFL has released the dates for every team's voluntary offseason workout program.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: As possible lockout looms, NFL has distributed 2026 crew assignments for regular officials
items.body_text: Clock keeps ticking toward expiation of current CBA.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Mike Vrabel attends Patriots season-ticket holder event
items.body_text: The situation involving Patriots coach Mike Vrabel has taken on a life of its own, due in large part to a string of questionable P.R. moves from the inception of the controversy.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Lions sign LB Joe Bachie, DL Jay Tufele
items.body_text: The Lions added a couple of veterans on the defensive side of the ball on Wednesday.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Report: Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby will check into treatment center for gambling addiction
items.body_text: As gambling becomes more legalized, normalized, and monetized, there will be issues.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Cardinals GM Monti Ossenfort: We'll see how much Carson Beck can handle
items.body_text: After the Cardinals drafted quarterback Carson Beck in the third round last Friday, General Manager Monti Ossenfort and head coach Mike LaFleur declined to say whether Beck will be in competition with Jacoby Brissett and Gardner Minshew to be the team's Week 1 starter.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Jets will not exercise Mazi Smith's option for 2027
items.body_text: The Jets announced on Wednesday that they have exercised their fifth-year option on edge rusher Will McDonald's contract, but they did not share their plans for defensive tackle Mazi Smith.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Chris Ballard: Colts will have patience while trying to trade Anthony Richardson
items.body_text: Colts General Manager Chris Ballard would like to trade quarterback Anthony Richardson, and he's willing to wait for an offer.
items.author: Michael David Smith

items.title: Panthers officially exercise Bryce Young's fifth-year option
items.body_text: Young is now set to make $25.904 million for the 2027 season.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Texans agree to three-year extension with LB Azeez Al-Shaair
items.body_text: The Cowboys had interest in a trade for Texans linebacker Azeez Al-Shaair before the draft.
items.author: Charean Williams

items.title: Fernando Mendoza will wear No. 15 with the blessing of Tom Flores
items.body_text: Before Tom Flores won two Super Bowls as the head coach of the Raiders, he played quarterback in Oakland for seven seasons.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Athletic editor Steven Ginsberg addresses Dianna Russini situation in all-hands meeting
items.body_text: When the photos of Patriots coach Mike Vrabel and reporter Dianna Russini first emerged 22 days ago, they were accompanied by three strong denials of impropriety — one from Vrabel, one from Russini, and one from Russini's employer, The Athletic.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: The Mark Sanchez criminal trial has been delayed again
items.body_text: Former NFL quarterback and Fox analyst Mark Sanchez faces criminal charges in Indianapolis arising from a fight with a 69-year-old truck driver.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Vikings launch search for new General Manager
items.body_text: After the Vikings made the belated decision to fire G.M.
items.author: Mike Florio

items.title: Report: Deshaun Watson emerged from Browns minicamp as favorite to be QB1
items.body_text: Watson missed all of 2025 after tearing his Achilles, but is back to compete for the job in 2026.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Jets exercise Will McDonald's fifth-year option
items.body_text: The Jets have made a decision about their option on edge rusher Will McDonald's contract for 2027.
items.author: Josh Alper

items.title: Ravens GM Eric DeCosta: We could trade for a center
items.body_text: Ravens General Manager Eric DeCosta said after the draft that it was "unfortunate" that the team wasn't able to add any help at center and that the cost of trading up to snag a prospect they liked was "probably prohibitive."
items.author: Josh Alper
